concepts.name: What is Biology
concepts.description: Biology as the science of life; major branches — botany, zoology, microbiology, physiology, ecology, genetics; scope and relevance of biology to medicine, agriculture and industry.

concepts.name: Characteristics of Living Organisms
concepts.description: Growth, reproduction, metabolism, cellular organisation, response to stimuli, homeostasis and evolution as the defining properties that distinguish living from non-living matter.

concepts.name: Need for Classification
concepts.description: Diversity of life and the rationale for systematic classification; identification, nomenclature and taxonomic hierarchy — kingdom to species.

concepts.name: Five Kingdom Classification
concepts.description: Whittaker's five-kingdom system — Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia; basis of classification — cell structure, body organisation, mode of nutrition, reproduction.

concepts.name: Binomial Nomenclature
concepts.description: Linnaean binomial system; rules of nomenclature — genus and species, italicisation, author citation; ICBN/ICZN conventions.

concepts.name: Viruses, Viroids and Lichens
concepts.description: Acellular nature of viruses; structure of a typical virus — capsid, genome; viroids as naked infectious RNA; lichens as symbiotic associations of algae and fungi.

concepts.name: Microscopy and Laboratory Techniques
concepts.description: Light vs electron microscopy; magnification and resolution; staining techniques; safe handling of laboratory glassware and specimens.

concepts.name: Levels of Biological Organisation
concepts.description: Hierarchy from molecules to biosphere — cell, tissue, organ, organ system, organism, population, community, ecosystem, biome, biosphere.

concepts.name: Cell Theory
concepts.description: Historical development of cell theory — Schleiden, Schwann, Virchow; the cell as the basic structural and functional unit of life.

concepts.name: Prokaryotic Cell Structure
concepts.description: Cell wall, plasma membrane, nucleoid, ribosomes, plasmids, flagella and pili in bacteria; absence of membrane-bound organelles.

concepts.name: Eukaryotic Cell Structure
concepts.description: Plant vs animal cell comparison; plasma membrane, cytoplasm, membrane-bound nucleus and organelles; compartmentalisation as an organisational principle.

concepts.name: Cell Membrane and Transport
concepts.description: Fluid mosaic model of the plasma membrane; passive transport — diffusion, osmosis, facilitated diffusion; active transport and the sodium-potassium pump; endocytosis and exocytosis.

concepts.name: Nucleus and Chromosomes
concepts.description: Nuclear envelope, nucleolus, chromatin; chromosome structure — centromere, telomere, chromatids; karyotype and chromosome number.

concepts.name: Mitochondria and Energy Organelles
concepts.description: Mitochondrial structure — outer/inner membrane, cristae, matrix; mitochondria as the site of aerobic respiration; semi-autonomous nature.

concepts.name: Chloroplast Structure
concepts.description: Double membrane, thylakoids, grana, stroma; chlorophyll location; chloroplast as the site of photosynthesis; endosymbiotic origin.

concepts.name: Endomembrane System
concepts.description: Endoplasmic reticulum (smooth and rough), Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, vacuoles; protein and lipid synthesis, modification, packaging and secretion pathways.

concepts.name: Cytoskeleton and Cell Motility
concepts.description: Microfilaments, microtubules and intermediate filaments; roles in shape, intracellular transport, cell division and motility — cilia and flagella.

concepts.name: The Cell Cycle
concepts.description: Interphase (G1, S, G2) and M phase; checkpoints and cyclin-CDK regulation; significance of regulated progression for growth and genome stability; oncogene activation and tumour-suppressor loss as pathological consequences of checkpoint failure.

concepts.name: Mitosis
concepts.description: Stages — prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase; spindle apparatus; significance in growth, repair and asexual reproduction.

concepts.name: Meiosis
concepts.description: Meiosis I and II; synapsis, crossing over, independent assortment; reductional vs equational division; significance for genetic variation and gamete formation.

concepts.name: Cell Signalling
concepts.description: Signal molecules and receptors; signal transduction pathways; second messengers; paracrine, endocrine and autocrine signalling.

concepts.name: Types of Biomolecules
concepts.description: Macromolecules vs micromolecules; carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, nucleic acids as the four major classes; elemental composition of living matter.

concepts.name: Carbohydrates and Lipids
concepts.description: Mono-, di- and polysaccharides; structural vs storage carbohydrates; fatty acids, triglycerides, phospholipids and steroids; roles in energy storage and membranes.

concepts.name: Proteins and Protein Structure
concepts.description: Amino acids and peptide bonds; primary, secondary, tertiary and quaternary structure; denaturation; structure-function relationship in enzymes and structural proteins; ubiquitin-proteasome degradation as the cell's mechanism for clearing misfolded proteins.

concepts.name: Enzymes and Enzyme Kinetics
concepts.description: Enzymes as biological catalysts; active site, substrate specificity, induced fit; factors affecting enzyme activity — temperature, pH, concentration; competitive and non-competitive inhibition.

concepts.name: Nucleic Acid Structure
concepts.description: Nucleotide composition — base, sugar, phosphate; DNA double helix (Watson-Crick model); RNA types — mRNA, tRNA, rRNA; base pairing rules.

concepts.name: DNA Replication
concepts.description: Semiconservative replication; helicase, DNA polymerase, primase, ligase; leading and lagging strands, Okazaki fragments; origin of replication.

concepts.name: Transcription
concepts.description: RNA polymerase and promoter recognition; initiation, elongation, termination; post-transcriptional processing — splicing, capping, polyadenylation in eukaryotes.

concepts.name: Translation and the Genetic Code
concepts.description: Codons and the genetic code — degeneracy, universality; ribosome structure and function; initiation, elongation, termination of translation; role of tRNA and aminoacyl synthetases.

concepts.name: Regulation of Gene Expression
concepts.description: Lac operon as a model of prokaryotic regulation; eukaryotic transcription factors, enhancers and silencers; epigenetic regulation — methylation, histone modification.

concepts.name: Mendelian Genetics
concepts.description: Mendel's laws of inheritance — dominance, segregation, independent assortment; monohybrid and dihybrid crosses; Punnett squares; test cross.

concepts.name: Gene Interactions and Extensions of Mendelism
concepts.description: Incomplete dominance, codominance, multiple alleles, epistasis, polygenic inheritance; deviations from simple Mendelian ratios.

concepts.name: Chromosomal Theory and Linkage
concepts.description: Chromosomal basis of inheritance; linkage and recombination frequency; gene mapping concepts; sex-linked inheritance and sex determination systems.

concepts.name: Pedigree Analysis and Human Genetic Disorders
concepts.description: Pedigree chart conventions; autosomal and sex-linked disorders — haemophilia, colour blindness, thalassemia, sickle cell anaemia; chromosomal disorders — Down, Turner, Klinefelter syndromes.

concepts.name: Mutations
concepts.description: Point mutations — substitution, insertion, deletion; frameshift vs non-frameshift effects; chromosomal mutations — duplication, deletion, inversion, translocation; mutagens.

concepts.name: Population Genetics
concepts.description: Allele and genotype frequencies; Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium and its assumptions; factors disturbing equilibrium — mutation, migration, selection, drift.

concepts.name: Genetic Engineering and Recombinant DNA
concepts.description: Restriction enzymes, vectors, cloning; PCR amplification; gene cloning vs gene expression; applications in producing recombinant proteins.

concepts.name: Origin of Life
concepts.description: Theories of the origin of life — Oparin-Haldane hypothesis, Miller-Urey experiment; prebiotic chemical evolution; RNA world hypothesis.

concepts.name: Evidence for Evolution
concepts.description: Fossil record, comparative anatomy — homologous and analogous structures, embryology, biogeography and molecular evidence supporting common descent.

concepts.name: Natural Selection and Darwinism
concepts.description: Darwin's theory of natural selection; variation, heritability, differential survival and reproduction; adaptation as a product of selection; industrial melanism as a case study.

concepts.name: Modern Synthesis and Speciation
concepts.description: Integration of Mendelian genetics with Darwinian selection; mechanisms of speciation — allopatric, sympatric; reproductive isolation; adaptive radiation.

concepts.name: Human Evolution
concepts.description: Primate evolutionary trends; major hominin fossils — Australopithecus, Homo habilis, Homo erectus, Homo sapiens; bipedalism and brain size trends.

concepts.name: Human Digestive System
concepts.description: Alimentary canal organisation; digestive glands; mechanical and chemical digestion; absorption of nutrients; common digestive disorders.

concepts.name: Human Respiratory System
concepts.description: Respiratory organs and mechanism of breathing; exchange and transport of gases — O2 and CO2; regulation of respiration; respiratory disorders.

concepts.name: Human Circulatory System
concepts.description: Structure of the heart and cardiac cycle; blood composition and blood groups; double circulation; lymphatic system; ECG basics; cardiovascular disorders.

concepts.name: Excretory System and Osmoregulation
concepts.description: Structure of the nephron; mechanism of urine formation — filtration, reabsorption, secretion; osmoregulation; role of ADH and the renin-angiotensin system.

concepts.name: Nervous System and Neural Control
concepts.description: Neuron structure; resting and action potentials; synaptic transmission; central, peripheral and autonomic nervous system organisation; reflex action.

concepts.name: Endocrine System
concepts.description: Major endocrine glands and their hormones; feedback regulation of hormone secretion; hormone mechanism of action — second messengers, gene activation.

concepts.name: Locomotion and Musculoskeletal System
concepts.description: Types of muscle tissue; sliding filament theory of muscle contraction; skeletal system organisation; joints and movement; disorders of muscles and bones.

concepts.name: Immune System Overview
concepts.description: Innate vs acquired immunity; humoral and cell-mediated immunity; structure of antibodies; lymphoid organs.

concepts.name: Photosynthesis
concepts.description: Light and dark reactions; photosystems I and II; electron transport and photophosphorylation; Calvin cycle (C3 pathway); C4 and CAM adaptations.

concepts.name: Respiration in Plants
concepts.description: Glycolysis, Krebs cycle and the electron transport chain; aerobic vs anaerobic respiration; respiratory quotient; relationship between photosynthesis and respiration.

concepts.name: Plant Water Relations
concepts.description: Absorption of water by roots; transpiration and the cohesion-tension theory; ascent of sap; stomatal regulation; water potential.

concepts.name: Mineral Nutrition in Plants
concepts.description: Essential macro- and micronutrients; deficiency symptoms; nitrogen fixation — biological and industrial; nutrient uptake mechanisms.

concepts.name: Plant Growth and Hormones
concepts.description: Auxins, gibberellins, cytokinins, abscisic acid and ethylene; phases of plant growth; photoperiodism and vernalisation.

concepts.name: Asexual Reproduction
concepts.description: Binary fission, budding, fragmentation, vegetative propagation; advantages and limitations compared to sexual reproduction.

concepts.name: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
concepts.description: Flower structure; microsporogenesis and megasporogenesis; pollination types; fertilisation and post-fertilisation events — endosperm and embryo development; seed and fruit formation.

concepts.name: Human Reproductive System
concepts.description: Male and female reproductive anatomy; gametogenesis — spermatogenesis and oogenesis; menstrual cycle and hormonal regulation.

concepts.name: Fertilisation and Embryonic Development
concepts.description: Fertilisation, cleavage, blastula and gastrula formation; implantation; placenta formation and function; parturition.

concepts.name: Reproductive Health and Contraception
concepts.description: Reproductive health awareness; methods of contraception; assisted reproductive technologies — IVF, ZIFT, GIFT; sexually transmitted infections.

concepts.name: Fundamentals of Animal Development
concepts.description: Stages from zygote to adult; cleavage patterns; cell fate determination — totipotency, pluripotency; significance of the embryonic axes.

concepts.name: Morphogenesis and Cell Differentiation
concepts.description: Induction and organiser regions; differential gene expression driving cell differentiation; morphogen gradients; classic experiments — Spemann organiser.

concepts.name: Stem Cells and Regeneration
concepts.description: Embryonic vs adult stem cells; pluripotency and induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs); regenerative capacity across species; therapeutic applications and ethical considerations.

concepts.name: Organisms and their Environment
concepts.description: Habitat and niche; abiotic and biotic factors; adaptations to climatic extremes; concept of acclimatisation.

concepts.name: Population Ecology
concepts.description: Population attributes — density, birth/death rate, age structure; population growth curves — exponential and logistic; population interactions — competition, predation, parasitism, mutualism.

concepts.name: Ecosystem Structure and Function
concepts.description: Producers, consumers and decomposers; food chains and food webs; ecological pyramids; energy flow and the 10% law.

concepts.name: Nutrient Cycling and Succession
concepts.description: Carbon, nitrogen and phosphorus cycles; primary and secondary ecological succession; climax communities.

concepts.name: Biodiversity and Conservation
concepts.description: Levels of biodiversity — genetic, species, ecosystem; measuring biodiversity; threats — habitat loss, invasive species, climate change; conservation strategies — in-situ and ex-situ.

concepts.name: Environmental Issues and Pollution
concepts.description: Air, water and soil pollution sources and effects; ozone depletion; eutrophication; global warming and climate change; sustainable development.

concepts.name: Microbial Diversity
concepts.description: Bacteria, archaea, fungi, protozoa and viruses; methods of classification and identification; ubiquity and ecological roles of microbes; uncultured diversity (estimated >99% of species) and the microbiome as host-associated microbial communities.

concepts.name: Microbial Growth and Culture Techniques
concepts.description: Bacterial growth curve — lag, log, stationary, death phases; culture media types; aseptic technique; sterilisation methods.

concepts.name: Microbes in Human Welfare
concepts.description: Role of microbes in food production — curd, bread, fermented beverages; antibiotics and their discovery; microbes in sewage treatment and biogas production.

concepts.name: Pathogenic Microorganisms and Disease
concepts.description: Common bacterial, viral, fungal and protozoan diseases in humans; modes of transmission; Koch's postulates; principles of disease control.

concepts.name: Innate and Adaptive Immunity
concepts.description: Physical, physiological and cellular barriers of innate immunity; humoral and cell-mediated branches of adaptive immunity; primary and secondary immune response.

concepts.name: Antibody Structure and Function
concepts.description: Immunoglobulin structure — heavy and light chains, variable and constant regions; antibody classes and their functions; antigen-antibody interaction specificity.

concepts.name: Vaccination and Immunisation
concepts.description: Active vs passive immunisation; types of vaccines — live attenuated, inactivated, subunit, mRNA; herd immunity concept; immunisation schedules.

concepts.name: Immune Disorders
concepts.description: Allergies and hypersensitivity reactions; autoimmune diseases; immunodeficiency — AIDS as a case study; organ transplant rejection and immunosuppression.

concepts.name: Principles of Biotechnology
concepts.description: Genetic engineering and bioprocess technology as the two core techniques; tools — restriction enzymes, vectors, host cells; basic bioreactor design.

concepts.name: Biotechnology Process Applications
concepts.description: Recombinant insulin and vaccine production; transgenic plants and Bt crops; gene therapy concepts; molecular diagnostics — ELISA, PCR-based detection.

concepts.name: Genomics and Proteomics
concepts.description: Genome sequencing strategies; the Human Genome Project; functional vs structural genomics; proteomics and the study of the complete protein complement of a cell.

concepts.name: CRISPR and Genome Editing
concepts.description: CRISPR-Cas9 mechanism — guide RNA, Cas9 nuclease, double-strand break repair; applications in gene knockout and correction; ethical and biosafety considerations.

concepts.name: Introduction to Bioinformatics
concepts.description: Biological databases — GenBank, UniProt, PDB; sequence file formats; the role of computation in modern biology.

concepts.name: Sequence Alignment
concepts.description: Pairwise alignment — global (Needleman-Wunsch) and local (Smith-Waterman); scoring matrices; multiple sequence alignment; BLAST search strategy.

concepts.name: Computational Phylogenetics
concepts.description: Distance-based and character-based tree-building methods; molecular clock hypothesis; interpreting phylogenetic trees from sequence data.

concepts.name: Structural Bioinformatics
concepts.description: Protein structure prediction approaches — homology modelling, ab initio methods; structural databases; relationship between sequence, structure and function.

concepts.name: Introduction to Systems Biology
concepts.description: Holistic vs reductionist approaches to biology; the concept of biological networks — gene regulatory, metabolic, signalling networks.

concepts.name: Gene Regulatory Networks
concepts.description: Network motifs — feedback and feedforward loops; modelling approaches for transcriptional regulation; emergent properties such as bistability and oscillation.

concepts.name: Metabolic Network Modelling
concepts.description: Flux balance analysis; stoichiometric modelling of metabolic pathways; constraint-based reconstruction of genome-scale metabolic networks.

concepts.name: Synthetic Biology
concepts.description: Engineering biological circuits from standardised parts; design-build-test cycle; applications in biosensing and biomanufacturing; biosafety considerations.

concepts.name: Three Domain System
concepts.description: Woese's three-domain classification from 16S rRNA phylogeny; distinguishing features of Bacteria, Archaea and Eukarya; Archaea-Eukarya phylogenetic proximity; molecular evidence for the three-domain tree of life.

concepts.name: Endosymbiotic Theory
concepts.description: Origin of mitochondria and chloroplasts from free-living bacteria; evidence: double membranes, 70S ribosomes, circular DNA, binary fission; primary and secondary endosymbiosis; genome reduction over evolutionary time; implications for the diversity and complexity of eukaryotic life.

concepts.name: Eukaryotic Supergroups and Protist Diversity
concepts.description: Major eukaryotic supergroups (SAR, Excavata, Amoebozoa, Archaeplastida); ecological significance of protists; endosymbiotic plastid origins in algal lineages; protists as ecological drivers and model organisms for cell biology.

concepts.name: Fungal Biology
concepts.description: Chitin cell walls; absorptive heterotrophy; hyphal growth and mycelium organisation; major fungal groups (Ascomycetes, Basidiomycetes, Zygomycetes, Chytridiomycetes); sexual and asexual reproduction; decomposer, pathogenic and mycorrhizal roles in ecosystems.

concepts.name: Plant Diversity and Alternation of Generations
concepts.description: Sporophyte and gametophyte alternation as the universal plant life cycle; trends across bryophytes, lycophytes, ferns, gymnosperms and angiosperms; structural adaptations to terrestrial life; progressive gametophyte reduction as a macro-evolutionary trend in land plants.

concepts.name: Cladistics and Phylogenetic Thinking
concepts.description: Synapomorphies as defining shared derived characters; monophyletic, paraphyletic and polyphyletic groups; parsimony and maximum likelihood as inference principles; reading phylogenetic trees (branch lengths, node support, outgroups); tree thinking as core quantitative literacy for all of biology.

concepts.name: Viral Replication and Lifecycle
concepts.description: Lytic and lysogenic cycles in bacteriophage; Baltimore classification of viruses by genome type (seven classes); attachment, entry, replication, assembly and release; retroviruses and reverse transcriptase; RNA-dependent RNA polymerase in RNA viruses.

concepts.name: Horizontal Gene Transfer
concepts.description: Transformation, transduction and conjugation as natural HGT mechanisms; HGT as the primary driver of prokaryotic genome evolution; antibiotic resistance spread as an applied consequence; the prokaryotic phylogeny as a reticulate network rather than a tree; evidence for ancient HGT events in eukaryotes.

concepts.name: Apoptosis and Programmed Cell Death
concepts.description: Intrinsic pathway (cytochrome c release, Bcl-2 family, apoptosome formation, caspase cascade); extrinsic pathway (death receptors, FADD, initiator caspases); executioner caspase activation and hallmark morphological changes; physiological roles in embryonic development, immune selection and homeostatic tissue turnover.

concepts.name: Epigenetics and Chromatin Regulation
concepts.description: DNA methylation and CpG island silencing; histone code hypothesis (acetylation, methylation, phosphorylation); chromatin remodelling complexes; heterochromatin versus euchromatin domains; genomic imprinting; X-chromosome inactivation (Barr body); evidence for transgenerational epigenetic inheritance.

concepts.name: Non-coding RNA Biology
concepts.description: siRNA and RISC-mediated RNA interference; miRNA biogenesis (Drosha, Dicer) and post-transcriptional repression; lncRNA roles in chromatin regulation and transcriptional control; piRNA and transposon silencing in the germline; ribozymes as catalytic RNA molecules.

concepts.name: Core Signal Transduction Pathways
concepts.description: MAPK/ERK cascade (RTK to Ras to Raf to MEK to ERK); Wnt/beta-catenin pathway (frizzled, Dishevelled, beta-catenin stabilisation, TCF/LEF transcription); Notch signalling (membrane-bound ligand, gamma-secretase cleavage, NICD nuclear translocation); JAK-STAT pathway; cross-pathway interactions and mechanisms of signalling specificity.

concepts.name: DNA Damage Response and Repair
concepts.description: Damage types (oxidative lesions, UV-induced pyrimidine dimers, single- and double-strand breaks); Base Excision Repair (glycosylase, AP endonuclease); Nucleotide Excision Repair (recognition, dual incision, gap-fill); Mismatch Repair (strand discrimination, nick-directed excision); Homologous Recombination versus NHEJ for DSBs; ATM/ATR checkpoint kinase activation.

concepts.name: Bioenergetics and Thermodynamics of Life
concepts.description: Gibbs free energy (delta G) and spontaneity; exergonic versus endergonic reactions and metabolic directionality; ATP as the universal energy currency; coupled reactions and energetic rationale; NAD+/NADH and FAD/FADH2 as electron carriers; chemiosmotic principle as the unifying theme of energy transduction.

concepts.name: Transposable Elements and Genome Organisation
concepts.description: DNA transposons (cut-and-paste via transposase); retrotransposons (copy-and-paste via RNA intermediate); LINEs, SINEs and LTR retrotransposons; transposable elements as approximately 50% of the human genome; the C-value paradox; genome organisation (coding, non-coding, repetitive elements, gene families, pseudogenes).

concepts.name: Molecular Evolution and Neutral Theory
concepts.description: Kimura's neutral theory and nearly-neutral theory; synonymous versus nonsynonymous substitutions; dN/dS ratio as a statistical test for natural selection; molecular clock hypothesis and its limitations; selective sweeps and their genomic signatures; purifying selection versus positive selection at the sequence level.

concepts.name: Evolutionary Developmental Biology
concepts.description: Hox genes as master regulators of body plan axis specification; genetic toolkit (conserved regulatory genes: Pax6, Distal-less, Tinman); deep homology across phyla; modularity and developmental constraints; heterochrony and heterotopy as mechanisms generating morphological variation across evolutionary time.

concepts.name: Community Ecology
concepts.description: Competitive exclusion principle (Gause's law) and coexistence mechanisms; character displacement and niche partitioning; keystone species and trophic cascades; intermediate disturbance hypothesis; succession (facilitation, tolerance, inhibition models); island biogeography theory; species diversity indices (Shannon, Simpson).

concepts.name: MHC and Antigen Presentation
concepts.description: MHC class I (all nucleated cells, endogenous peptides, CD8+ cytotoxic T cell activation); MHC class II (APCs, exogenous peptides, CD4+ helper T cell activation); antigen processing pathways (proteasome, TAP, lysosomal); peptide-MHC complex and TCR recognition geometry; self-tolerance via thymic positive and negative selection; MHC restriction; allograft rejection mechanism.
